methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pipedream is a Series A-stage transportation company founded by Garrett McCurrach and Canon Reeves that has raised $22.4M to date. The transportation sector comp universe consists of 8 private peers, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, FleetZero, and Parallel Systems, with no public comparables identified.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $22.4M raised proxy, yielding $44.8M. Peer-median triangulation was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow transportation set; three peers exist in the sector but none met the threshold of three or more datapoints after filtering for stage and valuation tier proximity. A peer-median approach anchored to broader sector data would introduce unacceptable noise given stage heterogeneity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $38M to $52M, with a median point of $45M. This range reflects a ±15% band around the stage-progression midpoint. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage transportation peers to validate the 2.0x multiplier, and limited visibility into comparable capital efficiency and market positioning within the comp set. Additional data on Series A outcomes in frontier transportation would strengthen conviction in this range.
